spec_match.rationale: The comparison accurately shows fixed margins clipping or wasting space while env(safe-printable-inset) adapts to the simulated printer margin, with a clear note separating screen fallback parsing from real print-context resolution.

interactive.rationale: The repaired native range input updates all three print strategies, status badges, explanations, and the comparison table for pointer and keyboard input.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page directly demonstrates the feature-index promise of adapting page margins to printer-reported unprintable areas.

edge_cases.rationale: The 2–22 mm range covers permissive, typical, and strict printer margins and explicitly distinguishes clipping, safe output, and wasted printable area.

references.rationale: The page links both ChromeStatus feature 5515971464527872 and the CSS Environment Variables safe-printable-inset specification.

conformance.rationale: The feature conformance route ran 6/6 assertions successfully in Headless Chrome 149, covering @page, env() parsing, screen fallback resolution, and canonical max() usage.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the slider at 8, 12, and 13 mm; visible and accessibility-tree readouts updated correctly, with no console errors and all network requests successful after repair.

telemetry_health.rationale: The repaired interaction completed cleanly and the page telemetry endpoint returned HTTP 202 without runtime, resource, console, or assertion failures.

design_system.rationale: The page uses the shared design-system variables and communicates safe, clipped, and wasteful outcomes with text as well as color.

accessibility.rationale: The native slider has the explicit accessible name “Printer's unprintable margin,” visible focus, arrow-key operation, and text equivalents that update alongside the visual page diagrams.

summary: DevTools MCP reproduced the broken inline oninput handler (ReferenceError: update is not defined). The handler was moved to addEventListener inside the script scope and the slider was given a precise accessible name. Pointer-style fill and ArrowRight keyboard input now update every comparison readout; console and network are clean, and conformance passes 6/6.
